summary:
Alpha-fetoprotein reaches its maximum concentration in fetal serum just before birth due to high production by the developing fetus; post-birth levels decline rapidly as this specific organ function ceases unless there are pathological reasons for elevation. To summarize concisely yet comprehensively based solely on your request about focusing only on critical elements related directly answering "Maximum level," we can say:

The highest concentrations of alpha-fetoprotein naturally occur within fetal blood shortly preceding delivery when produced primarily at a very high rate from the growing fetal liver tissue necessary for normal embryonic growth processes. Afterbirth, significant drops follow indicating cessation or redirection away from such intense hepatic activity typical prenatally but not required afterwards without disease states causing abnormal
